seo.title: Orange duck - so fancy after all - Marinda Kook

html:
When we were small we didn't know food like orange duck. It was completely foreign to us. Only once I left the farm and came to discover the city did I realise you can eat duck too – it's not just for swimming in the farm dam. I slow-cooked the duck and served it with jasmine rice.

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 1 duck
  • 4 potatoes
  • 1 orange
  • 250 ml orange juice
  • 100 ml marmalade or apricot jam (marmalade is nicer)
  • 5 ml vinegar
  • 100 ml chicken stock powder (you won't use it all at once)
  • 100 ml cake flour
  • 100 ml brown sugar
  • 250 ml warm water
  • 10 ml cornflour (mazina)
  • salt and pepper
  • 125 ml Van der Hum liqueur (or any other orange liqueur)

NOW:

  1. wash the duck inside and out and snip off any loose bits of skin – remember to remove the little bag of giblets inside (cook it up for the animals)
  2. pat the duck dry with a cloth
  3. halve the orange and press it into the duck
  4. peel your potatoes and halve them, place them in the bottom of an oven dish in which you'll cook the duck
  5. put a wire rack in the dish, because you're going to sit the duck on the wire rack.
  6. mix the flour, salt and pepper and 50 ml of the chicken stock powder in a bowl, then rub the whole duck with it
  7. place the duck on the rack in the dish and prick the duck skin with a knife – the fat needs to run out (nothing makes better roast potatoes than duck fat)
  8. place in an oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 2 hours
  9. don't cover the duck, and after an hour turn the duck over – it needs to brown nicely all over
  10. in the meantime make the sauce: pour the orange juice, vinegar, water, marmalade, brown sugar, chicken stock and water into a pot and simmer gently for 10 minutes
  11. mix the cornflour with 80 ml of tap water to a paste and stir it into your orange syrup – cook for 5 minutes

Take your glossy, fatty duck out of the oven and enjoy it with your jasmine rice and potatoes. It takes a bit of time and effort but every bite is worth it.
